question: [File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getFileData()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an arbitrary fileId and timestamp to getFileData() with no caller-side authorization check, causing the service to return the aggregated file_data of any file entity on the ledger without verifying that the underlying FILECREATE(17) or FILEUPDATE(19) transactions were signed by the file's key list, leading to unauthorized read of sensitive file contents?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getLatestFileDataContents()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger getLatestFileDataContents() for any fileId via the /contracts/results/:timestamp API path, causing the service to aggregate and return file_data from FILEAPPEND(16), FILECREATE(17), and FILEUPDATE(19) transactions without verifying that any of those transactions carried valid signatures from the file's authorized key list, resulting in unauthorized disclosure of file contents?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user call the public /network/exchangerate endpoint to trigger getExchangeRate(), which internally calls fallbackRetry() and then getLatestFileDataContents() against the system exchange rate file, without any check that the FILECREATE(17) or FILEUPDATE(19) transactions that produced the stored file_data were authorized by the system file's key holders, allowing an attacker to observe whether unauthorized exchange rate updates were accepted?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: fallbackRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that causes fallbackRetry() to iterate up to 10 times through getLatestFileDataContents() calls, each time serving progressively older file versions, none of which are checked for authorization by the file's signers/owners, meaning the service may ultimately serve a file version that was written by an unauthorized transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getLatestFileContentsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the latestFileContentsQuery inner CTE filters on transaction_type IN (17, 19) while the outer query includes transaction_type IN (16, 17, 19), meaning FILEAPPEND(16) chunks are aggregated via string_agg without any per-chunk verification that the append transaction was authorized by the file's key list, allowing unauthorized append content to be silently included in the returned file_data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getLatestFileContentsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getLatestFileContentsQuery() uses innerWhere.replaceAll('and ', 'and f.') to construct outerWhere, such that if innerWhere contains the substring 'and ' in a position other than a SQL keyword boundary (e.g., inside a string literal or column alias), the replacement produces malformed SQL that bypasses the transaction_type filter, allowing unauthorized transaction types to be included in the file_data aggregation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: buildWhereSqlStatement()] Can an unprivileged external user, by influencing the whereQuery array passed to buildWhereSqlStatement() via API query parameters, inject a current.query string that is interpolated directly into the SQL without parameterization (only current.param is parameterized), causing the WHERE clause to omit or override the transaction_type filter and return file_data from unauthorized transaction types?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getFileData()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp value of 0 or a very large integer to getFileData(), causing the getFileDataQuery subquery to anchor on the earliest or latest FILECREATE(17)/FILEUPDATE(19) record regardless of whether that record was authorized by the file's key holders, and then aggregate all subsequent FILEAPPEND(16) chunks including any that were written without proper signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getLatestFileDataContents()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an empty whereQuery array ({whereQuery: []}) to getLatestFileDataContents(), causing buildWhereSqlStatement() to return an empty WHERE extension and the latestFileContentsQuery to select the absolute latest file version with no timestamp bound, serving file_data from the most recent FILECREATE/FILEUPDATE/FILEAPPEND transactions without any check that those transactions were signed by the file's authorized key list?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: fallbackRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger fallbackRetry() with a filterQueries object where whereQuery is null or undefined, causing the nullish coalescing operator (whereQuery ?? []) to default to an empty array, meaning no timestamp constraint is applied and the service serves the latest file version without verifying that the underlying transactions were authorized by the file's signers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getLatestFileContentsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the latestFileContentsQuery uses string_agg(f.file_data, '' ORDER BY f.consensus_timestamp) to concatenate all file chunks since the latest FILECREATE/FILEUPDATE anchor, with no per-row check that each chunk's transaction was authorized by the file's key list, meaning a single unauthorized FILEAPPEND(16) transaction in the database will be silently included in the returned file_data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getFileData()] Can an unprivileged external user call getFileData() with a fileId corresponding to a system file (e.g., the exchange rate file or fee schedule file), causing the service to return the raw binary contents of that system file without verifying that the FILECREATE(17) or FILEUPDATE(19) transactions that wrote those contents were authorized by the system file's key holders?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call getExchangeRate() with different timestamp filter values to enumerate historical exchange rate file versions, each served without any check that the FILECREATE(17) or FILEUPDATE(19) transactions that produced those versions were authorized by the system file's key holders, allowing the attacker to reconstruct the full history of potentially unauthorized exchange rate changes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: fallbackRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where fallbackRetry() encounters a row where row is non-null but new resultConstructor(row) throws an error, causing the catch block to access row.consensus_timestamp and row.first_consensus_timestamp for logging and filter construction, and then append a new whereQuery entry using FileData.CONSENSUS_TIMESTAMP + utils.opsMap.lt without verifying that the previous file version at first_consensus_timestamp was authorized by the file's signers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getLatestFileContentsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the inner CTE (latest_create) selects max(consensus_timestamp) from file_data where transaction_type IN (17, 19), meaning a FILEUPDATE(19) transaction with empty file_data (length = 0) can still serve as the anchor for the outer aggregation, allowing subsequent FILEAPPEND(16) chunks from potentially unauthorized transactions to be included in the returned file_data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getFileData()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a fileId that belongs to another user's file entity and a timestamp that predates any authorization revocation, causing getFileData() to return the file_data aggregated from FILECREATE(17)/FILEUPDATE(19)/FILEAPPEND(16) transactions that may have been submitted without the file owner's approval, since the query only filters by entity_id and transaction_type with no signer verification?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getLatestFileDataContents()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger getLatestFileDataContents() via the /contracts/results/:transactionIdOrHash endpoint by submitting a transaction hash that resolves to an Ethereum transaction with a callDataId pointing to a file entity, causing the service to return that file's aggregated contents without verifying that the FILECREATE/FILEUPDATE/FILEAPPEND transactions were authorized by the file's key list?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getLatestFileContentsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the outer query in latestFileContentsQuery joins on f.consensus_timestamp >= l.consensus_timestamp with no upper bound other than the optional filterOuterPlaceholder, meaning if the filterOuterPlaceholder is empty (no timestamp filter), all FILEAPPEND(16) transactions after the latest FILECREATE/FILEUPDATE anchor are included regardless of whether they were authorized by the file's signers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: buildWhereSqlStatement()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a whereQuery entry where current.query contains SQL keywords or operators that are not sanitized before being interpolated into the query string (e.g., 'consensus_timestamp < 1 OR 1=1 --'), causing the WHERE clause to be bypassed and returning file_data from transactions that were not authorized by the file's key holders?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getFileData()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that getFileDataQuery uses a correlated subquery to find the most recent FILECREATE(17) or non-empty FILEUPDATE(19) anchor before timestamp $2, and then aggregates all file_data up to $2, with no check that the anchor transaction or any subsequent FILEAPPEND(16) was authorized by the file's key list, meaning the service treats all recorded transactions as implicitly authorized?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getLatestFileContentsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the latestFileContentsQuery groups by f.entity_id and returns a single aggregated row, and that getSingleRow() returns null if rows.length > 1, meaning if an attacker can cause the GROUP BY to produce multiple rows (e.g., by manipulating entity_id encoding), the service returns null and falls back to a previous file version that may also be unauthorized?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: fallbackRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the fallbackRetry() loop 10 times by causing each resultConstructor instantiation to throw, forcing the service to serve progressively older file versions, each of which may have been written by transactions that lacked proper authorization from the file's key holders, ultimately returning the oldest available version regardless of its authorization status?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user call getExchangeRate() with a timestamp filter that selects a specific historical exchange rate version, causing the service to return exchange rate data from a FILECREATE(17) or FILEUPDATE(19) transaction that was recorded in the database without verification that the transaction was signed by the exchange rate file's authorized key holders?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getLatestFileContentsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the filterInnerPlaceholder is replaced using String.prototype.replace() (not replaceAll), meaning only the first occurrence of the placeholder is replaced, and if the latestFileContentsQuery template were ever to contain the placeholder string twice, the second occurrence would remain as a literal string in the SQL, potentially causing a syntax error that bypasses the transaction_type authorization filter?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getLatestFileDataContents()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a whereQuery array with multiple entries to getLatestFileDataContents(), causing buildWhereSqlStatement() to join them with 'and', and then getLatestFileContentsQuery() to prepend 'f.' to each 'and ' occurrence via replaceAll, potentially producing a malformed outer WHERE clause that drops the transaction_type IN (16,17,19) filter and returns file_data from unauthorized transaction types?
